20	// parkCountingStore wraps claimCountingStore and counts ParkToProposed calls.
21	type parkCountingStore struct {

23		parkCalls int32
24	}
25	
26	func (s *parkCountingStore) ParkToProposed(id string, reason bead.ParkReason, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error {
27		atomic.AddInt32(&s.parkCalls, 1)
28		return s.Store.ParkToProposed(id, reason, mutate)
29	}
30	
31	func (s *parkCountingStore) ParkToProposedWithIntakeEvent(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail string, body map[string]any, at time.Time, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error {
32		atomic.AddInt32(&s.parkCalls, 1)
33		return s.Store.ParkToProposedWithIntakeEvent(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail, body, at, mutate)
34	}

4013			return false, fmt.Errorf("pre-claim intake park requires a bead candidate")

4024		findingFingerprint := preClaimIntakeFindingFingerprint(candidate, ruleID, reason, "pre_claim_intake", "block", "park", "review intake result and accept, rewrite, split, block, or cancel")
4025		promptFingerprint := bead.PromptFingerprint(*candidate)
4026		if honored, err := preClaimIntakeOverrideHonored(store, candidate, findingFingerprint); err != nil {
4027			return false, err
4028		} else if honored {
4029			appendPreClaimIntakeOverrideHonoredEvent(store, candidate.ID, actor, findingFingerprint, promptFingerprint, at)

64	// ParkToProposedWithIntakeEvent transitions a bead to proposed status and
65	// appends an intake.blocked event. It atomically performs the transition and
66	// appends the event. The mutate callback runs after the status transition and
67	// before the event is appended; pass nil if no additional mutations are needed.
68	func (s *Store) ParkToProposedWithIntakeEvent(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail string, body map[string]any, at time.Time, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
69		// Transition to proposed with intake rejection
70		if err := s.ParkToProposed(id, ParkIntakeRejection, mutate); err != nil {
71			return err
72		}
73	
74		// Append the intake.blocked event
75		bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(body)
76		return s.AppendEvent(id, BeadEvent{
77			Kind:      "intake.blocked",
78			Summary:   outcome,
79			Body:      string(bodyJSON),
80			Actor:     actor,
81			Source:    "ddx work",
82			CreatedAt: at,
83		})
84	}

115	func TestIntake_HookRunsAfterClaim(t *testing.T) {
116		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
117		var preflightSeen int32
118		var intakeSeen int32
119		store := &claimCountingStore{
120			Store: inner,
121			beforeClaim: func() {
122				if atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen) == 0 {
123					t.Fatal("route preflight must run before Claim")
124				}
125				// Claim now happens BEFORE intake: intake must NOT have run yet.
126				if atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeSeen) != 0 {
127					t.Fatal("Claim must run before PreClaimIntakeHook")
128				}
129			},
130		}
131	
132		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
133			Store: store,
134			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
135				return ExecuteBeadReport{
136					BeadID:    beadID,
137					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
138					SessionID: "sess-intake-order",
139					ResultRev: "fedcba",
140				}, nil
141			}),
142		}
143	
144		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker", Harness: "claude", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"}
145		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
146	
147		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
148			Once: true,
149			RoutePreflight: func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
150				atomic.StoreInt32(&preflightSeen, 1)
151				return nil
152			},
153			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
154				if atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen) == 0 {
155					t.Fatal("PreClaimIntakeHook must run after route preflight")
156				}
157				if atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls) == 0 {
158					t.Fatal("PreClaimIntakeHook must run after Claim")
159				}
160				atomic.StoreInt32(&intakeSeen, 1)
161				return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
162			},
163		})
164		require.NoError(t, err)
165		require.NotNil(t, result)
166	
167		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen), "route preflight must run")
168		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeSeen), "intake hook must run")
169		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "Claim must run before intake")
170	}

172	func TestIntake_NonAtomicSkipsClaim(t *testing.T) {
173		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
174		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
175	
176		var intakeCalls int32

178			Store: store,
179			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
180				t.Fatal("executor must not run when intake is non-atomic")
181				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
182			}),
183		}
184	
185		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
186		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
187	
188		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
189			Once:         true,
190			TargetBeadID: "ddx-0001",
191			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
192				atomic.AddInt32(&intakeCalls, 1)

198		})

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(workingDir), 0o755))
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "backend,urgent", "--acceptance", "bug is fixed")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	createdID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, createdID)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"))

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var created map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &created))
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, created["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, "Fix auth bug", created["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", created["issue_type"])
	assert.Equal(t, "open", created["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), created["priority"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", createdID, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me", "--labels", "backend")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", updated["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", updated["owner"])
	require.Len(t, updated["labels"], 1)

	listOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "list", "--status", "in_progress", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var listed []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &listed))
	require.Len(t, listed, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, listed[0]["id"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", createdID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	statusOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var status map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(statusOut), &status))
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["closed"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), status["open"])
}

func TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Transition target", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "blocked")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "transition_requires_external_blocker")

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var got map[string]any

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestBeadShowSurfacesFingerprintAndOverride asserts `ddx bead show` renders
// rule_fingerprint and operator_override for each intake.blocked entry.
func TestBeadShowSurfacesFingerprintAndOverride(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	// Create a bead
	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Test bead", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	beadID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	// Get the bead store and manually add an intake.blocked event
	s := factory.beadStore()
	b, err := s.Get(beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Create intake.blocked event with rule_fingerprint and operator_override
	ruleFingerprint := "abc123def456"
	operatorOverride := false
	eventBody := map[string]any{
		"rule_id":           "pre_claim_intake.operator_required",
		"rule_fingerprint":  ruleFingerprint,
		"operator_override": operatorOverride,
		"reason":            "test_reason",
		"detail":            "test detail",
	}
	bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(eventBody)

	err = s.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
		Kind:      "intake.blocked",
		Summary:   "test_outcome",
		Body:      string(bodyJSON),
		Actor:     "test_actor",
		Source:    "test",
		CreatedAt: b.CreatedAt.UTC(),
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Now show the bead and check for intake.blocked display
	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "IntakeBlocked:", "output must contain IntakeBlocked section")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "rule_id:", "output must display rule_id")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "rule_fingerprint:", "output must display rule_fingerprint")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, ruleFingerprint, "output must contain the rule_fingerprint value")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "operator_override:", "output must display operator_override")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "false", "output must display operator_override value")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "reason:", "output must display reason")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "test_reason", "output must contain the reason value")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "detail:", "output must display detail")
	assert.Contains(t, showOut, "test detail", "output must contain the detail value")
}

// TestBeadShowIntakeBlockedJSON asserts JSON output includes intake.blocked events
// with all fields.
func TestBeadShowIntakeBlockedJSON(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	// Create a bead
	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Test bead JSON", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	beadID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	// Get the bead store and manually add an intake.blocked event
	s := factory.beadStore()
	b, err := s.Get(beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Create intake.blocked event with rule_fingerprint and operator_override
	ruleFingerprint := "xyz789abc"
	operatorOverride := true
	eventBody := map[string]any{
		"rule_id":           "pre_claim_intake.timeout",
		"rule_fingerprint":  ruleFingerprint,
		"operator_override": operatorOverride,
		"reason":            "timeout",
		"detail":            "readiness timeout",
	}
	bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(eventBody)

	err = s.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
		Kind:      "intake.blocked",
		Summary:   "timeout_outcome",
		Body:      string(bodyJSON),
		Actor:     "test_actor",
		Source:    "test",
		CreatedAt: b.CreatedAt.UTC(),
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Now show the bead with JSON output
	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", beadID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var result map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &result))

	// Verify events are included
	events, ok := result["events"].([]any)
	require.True(t, ok, "result must include events array")

	// Find intake.blocked event
	var intakeBlockedEvent map[string]any
	for _, ev := range events {
		if eventMap, ok := ev.(map[string]any); ok {
			if kind, ok := eventMap["kind"].(string); ok && kind == "intake.blocked" {
				intakeBlockedEvent = eventMap
				break
			}
		}
	}
	require.NotNil(t, intakeBlockedEvent, "intake.blocked event must be in events")

	// Verify event body contains the fields
	bodyStr, ok := intakeBlockedEvent["body"].(string)
	require.True(t, ok)

	var eventBodyObj map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(bodyStr), &eventBodyObj))

	assert.Equal(t, ruleFingerprint, eventBodyObj["rule_fingerprint"], "rule_fingerprint must be present")
	assert.Equal(t, operatorOverride, eventBodyObj["operator_override"], "operator_override must be true")
}
